fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 62 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when the number of recent events exactly equals the limit the gate incorrectly allows the event instead of blocking it.

constraints.intent: When recent count equals limit the condition must evaluate to True (blocking), which fails with strict >.
Verify that when recent count equals limit the gate should block; the > operator misses this boundary case.
In the else branch (allowed=True), recent count must be strictly less than limit; if equal to limit we should not reach here.
A return of (True, 0) is contradictory — allowed but zero remaining — which can happen when count equals limit with the wrong operator.
